2604.19278.title: Explicit Trait Inference for Multi-Agent Coordination
2604.19278.paper_summary: LLM-based multi-agent systems (MAS) show promise on complex tasks but remain prone to coordination failures such as goal drift, error cascades, and misaligned behaviors. We propose Explicit Trait Inference (ETI), a psychologically grounded method for improving coordination. ETI enables agents to infer and track partner characteristics along two established psychological dimensions--warmth (e.g., trust) and competence (e.g., skill)--from interaction histories to guide decisions. We evaluate ETI in controlled settings (economic games), where it reduces payoff loss by 45-77%, and in more realistic, complex multi-agent settings (MultiAgentBench), where it improves performance by 3-29% depending on the scenario and model, relative to a CoT baseline. Additional analysis shows that gains are closely linked to trait inference: ETI profiles predict agents' actions, and informative profiles drive improvements. These results highlight ETI as a lightweight and robust mechanism for improving coordination in diverse multi-agent settings, and provide the first systematic evidence that LLM agents can (i) reliably infer others' traits from interaction histories and (ii) leverage structured awareness of others' traits for coordination.
2604.19278.paper_summary_zh: LLM 基於的多代理系統 (MAS) 在複雜任務上展現出潛力，但仍然容易出現協調失敗，例如目標漂移、錯誤級聯和行為不一致。我們提出了明確特徵推斷 (ETI)，這是一種基於心理學的方法，用於改善協調。ETI 使代理能夠從互動歷史中推斷和追蹤夥伴特徵，沿著兩個已建立的心理學維度——溫暖（例如，信任）和能力（例如，技能）——來指導決策。我們在受控環境（經濟遊戲）中評估 ETI，結果顯示其減少了 45-77% 的收益損失，並在更現實的複雜多代理環境（MultiAgentBench）中，根據情境和模型，相對於 CoT 基準，性能提高了 3-29%。額外分析顯示，增益與特徵推斷密切相關：ETI 檔案預測代理的行動，而有資訊的檔案則促進了改進。這些結果突顯了 ETI 作為一種輕量且穩健的機制，用於改善多樣化多代理環境中的協調，並提供了第一個系統性證據，表明 LLM 代理可以 (i) 可靠地從互動歷史中推斷他人的特徵，以及 (ii) 利用對他人特徵的結構化認知來進行協調。
2604.19278.author: Suhaib Abdurahman et.al.
2604.19278.authors: Suhaib Abdurahman, Etsuko Ishii, Katerina Margatina, Divya Bhargavi, Monica Sunkara, Yi Zhang
